title: Gymnast Nadia Comăneci Sticks the Landing
story: Flipping through the air, a young gymnast made history on July 18. In 1976, Nadia Comăneci wowed the world at the Montreal Olympics. Her incredible performances changed gymnastics forever and inspired countless young athletes.

title: Jupiter's Cosmic Bruise
story: Boom! A comet smashed into Jupiter, leaving a massive dark mark. On July 18, 1994, the largest fragment of Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 slammed into the gas giant. The impact created a black spot on Jupiter's surface, stretching about as wide as Earth itself! Astronomers watched in awe as this cosmic collision unfolded, giving us a rare glimpse of planetary defense in action.

title: Scientist's Brave Self-Test Fights Cholera
story: Waldemar Haffkine, a Ukrainian scientist, made a daring move in the battle against cholera. On July 18, 1892, he became the first person to test a cholera vaccine on a human - himself! title: Pitching Perfection: David Cone's Flawless Game
story: Imagine throwing a baseball so well that not a single batter reaches base. That's exactly what David Cone did for the New York Yankees on July 18, 1999. story: Burt Kwouk, the actor who brought laughter to millions in the Pink Panther movies, entered the world on July 18, 1930. Born in Warrington, England, Kwouk would grow up to become a beloved figure in comedy. His hilarious performances as Cato, Inspector Clouseau's loyal but mischievous servant, delighted audiences for years.
activity_question: In which movie series did Burt Kwouk become famous?
bonus_fact: Burt Kwouk's character Cato was known for surprise-attacking Inspector Clouseau to keep him alert.

title: A Champion for Civil Rights
story: Stephen Gill Spottswood entered the world in Boston on July 18, 1897. He grew up to become a powerful voice for equality. As a bishop in the African Methodist Episcopal Zion Church, Spottswood used his position to fight for civil rights. He even led the NAACP, a major organization working to end racial discrimination, for over a decade.
activity_question: What position did Stephen Gill Spottswood hold in the NAACP?
follow_up_question: Why do you think religious leaders often become involved in civil rights movements?

title: The Musical Genius Behind Thunderbirds
story: Barry Gray, born in 1908, was a musical wizard who brought TV shows to life. This talented British composer created the thrilling sounds for Thunderbirds and Space:1999. Gray mastered many instruments, including the piano, Hammond organ, and the eerie-sounding ondes Martenot. His music made space adventures feel real to viewers on July 18 and beyond.
activity_question: What TV show did Barry Gray compose music for?
bonus_fact: The ondes Martenot is an early electronic instrument that makes spooky, otherworldly sounds.
